source_rationale: RGS5 is a canonical marker of pericytes and vascular mural cells, highly expressed in this cluster
ACTA2 (alpha smooth muscle actin) is a hallmark of pericytes and smooth muscle cells
Transgelin is a smooth muscle-specific cytoskeletal protein marking pericytes/vascular smooth muscle cells
PDGFRB is a key receptor tyrosine kinase and definitive marker of pericytes
NOTCH3 is a signaling receptor specifically expressed in pericytes and vascular mural cells
MYH11 (smooth muscle myosin heavy chain) marks contractile smooth muscle/pericyte identity
MCAM (CD146) is a cell adhesion molecule enriched in pericytes
Phospholamban is expressed in smooth muscle cells and pericytes regulating calcium signaling
CSPG4 (NG2) is a classical pericyte surface marker
Connexin 37 is expressed in vascular mural cells and pericytes
CD3D is a core component of the T cell receptor complex, defining T lymphocytes
CD8A marks cytotoxic T lymphocytes
Granzyme K is characteristic of effector memory CD8+ T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule protein expressed in activated T cells and NK cells
CCL5 (RANTES) is highly expressed by activated effector T cells
Granzyme A is a serine protease in cytotoxic T lymphocyte granules
CD3E is part of the TCR complex confirming T cell identity
PTPRC (CD45) is a pan-leukocyte marker confirming immune cell identity
Interferon gamma is a key cytokine produced by activated cytotoxic T cells
Perforin is essential for cytotoxic T cell killing function
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor expressed on cytotoxic T cells and NK cells
Decorin is a proteoglycan highly expressed by stromal fibroblasts
PDGFRA is a canonical marker of fibroblasts and mesenchymal stromal cells
Type I collagen alpha-1 is a major extracellular matrix gene produced by fibroblasts
Type I collagen alpha-2 chain is a hallmark fibroblast product
Serine protease inhibitor expressed by ovarian stromal fibroblasts
TCF21 is a transcription factor marking ovarian theca/stromal cells
FOXL2 is a key transcription factor in ovarian granulosa and stromal cell identity
Osteoglycin is a proteoglycan enriched in stromal fibroblasts
Matrix metalloproteinase 2 is produced by fibroblasts for ECM remodeling
Steroidogenic acute regulatory protein indicates steroidogenic stromal cells of the ovary
Von Willebrand factor is a definitive endothelial cell marker
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein specific to vascular endothelial cells
VE-cadherin (CDH5) is a specific endothelial adherens junction protein
CD34 is a sialomucin expressed on vascular endothelial cells
KDR (VEGFR2) is the primary VEGF receptor on endothelial cells
ERG is an ETS transcription factor essential for endothelial cell identity
Endomucin is a sialomucin specifically expressed on vascular endothelium
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor marking endothelial cells
EGFL7 is an endothelial-specific secreted factor involved in vascular development
ICAM2 is constitutively expressed on vascular endothelial cells
Anti-Müllerian hormone is produced specifically by ovarian granulosa cells
FOXL2 is the master transcription factor of granulosa cell differentiation
Inhibin alpha subunit is a hallmark granulosa cell product
17-beta hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase 1 is involved in estrogen biosynthesis in granulosa cells
Follistatin is produced by granulosa cells to regulate activin signaling
WFDC2 (HE4) is expressed in ovarian epithelial and granulosa cells
Cytokeratin 18 marks epithelial-like granulosa cells
Desmoplakin indicates cell-cell junctions characteristic of granulosa cells
AMH receptor type 2 is specifically expressed on granulosa cells
Prostaglandin D2 synthase is highly expressed in granulosa cells
WT1 transcription factor is expressed in ovarian granulosa cells
Complement C1q subunit A is a marker of tissue-resident macrophages
CD14 is a monocyte/macrophage lineage marker
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein in myeloid cells
AIF1 (IBA1) is a macrophage/microglia-specific calcium-binding protein
Lysozyme is a classic monocyte/macrophage marker
Fc epsilon receptor gamma chain is expressed on myeloid cells
CD68 is a lysosomal glycoprotein characteristic of macrophages
High-affinity IgE receptor alpha chain suggests dendritic cell or mast cell component
CCL3 (MIP-1alpha) is a chemokine produced by activated macrophages
SPI1 (PU.1) is a master transcription factor for myeloid cell differentiation
CD3D co-expression with myeloid markers suggests a mixed immune cluster with T cell component
CD163 is a scavenger receptor specific to tissue-resident macrophages
Complement C1q is highly expressed by tissue-resident macrophages
C1QB complement component marks resident macrophages
Colony stimulating factor 1 receptor is essential for macrophage differentiation
LYVE1 marks tissue-resident macrophages, particularly perivascular macrophages
Folate receptor beta marks tissue-resident anti-inflammatory macrophages
Stabilin-1 is a scavenger receptor on tissue-resident macrophages
Mannose receptor family member marks M2-like tissue macrophages
MS4A6A is a membrane-spanning protein enriched in macrophages
Coagulation factor XIII A chain is expressed by tissue-resident macrophages
MERTK is a receptor tyrosine kinase essential for macrophage efferocytosis
Zona pellucida glycoprotein 3 is an oocyte-specific gene
FIGLA is an oocyte-specific transcription factor essential for folliculogenesis
DAZL is an RNA-binding protein essential for germ cell development
DDX4 (VASA) is a definitive germ cell marker
Synaptonemal complex protein 3 marks meiotic germ cells
NLRP5 is a maternal-effect gene specifically expressed in oocytes
DPPA3 (Stella) is an oocyte and primordial germ cell marker
LHX8 is a transcription factor required for oocyte development
SOHLH2 is a germ cell-specific transcription factor essential for oogenesis
STAG3 is a meiosis-specific cohesin subunit expressed in oocytes
TUBB8 is an oocyte-specific beta-tubulin isotype
RGS5 is the top marker, indicating pericytes/vascular smooth muscle cells similar to cluster 1
Alpha smooth muscle actin marks pericytes and smooth muscle cells
Smooth muscle myosin heavy chain indicates mature smooth muscle/pericyte identity
Transgelin is a smooth muscle cytoskeletal marker
NOTCH3 signaling receptor is specific to pericytes and vascular mural cells
PDGFRB is a definitive pericyte marker
Phospholamban marks smooth muscle/pericyte contractile cells
Calponin 1 is a smooth muscle-specific contractile protein
MCAM (CD146) is a pericyte surface marker
Caldesmon is a smooth muscle-specific actin-binding protein distinguishing this from cluster 1
Decorin is a hallmark proteoglycan of stromal fibroblasts
PDGFRA marks fibroblasts and mesenchymal stromal cells
Type I collagen is the primary ECM product of fibroblasts
Osteoglycin is enriched in stromal fibroblasts
TCF21 is a transcription factor marking ovarian theca/stromal cells
FOXL2 marks ovarian stromal and granulosa lineage cells
Lumican is a proteoglycan produced by fibroblasts, distinguishing this stromal subpopulation
CXCL12 (SDF-1) is a chemokine produced by stromal fibroblasts
APOE is expressed by stromal cells involved in lipid metabolism
PAI-1 is produced by activated stromal fibroblasts
